notes_md:
**có** usually means **“have”**. You use it to say someone possesses or owns something.

It also means **“there is / there are”** when talking about existence.  

For negatives, add **không** before it: **không có** = “don’t have / there isn’t.”  
For yes–no questions, put **có … không?** around the main verb, or just raise your tone with **có**.  

Memory tip: think of **có** as the opposite of **không có** — “have” vs. “not have / no.”

content.sentence: Chúng ta ___ thời gian để học.

example.vi: Tôi có một người bạn tốt.

definitions.en: A word used to indicate possession or the existence of something.
definitions.vi: Từ dùng để chỉ sự sở hữu hoặc sự tồn tại của một điều gì đó.

notes:
có usually means “have”. You use it to say someone possesses or owns something.

It also means “there is / there are” when talking about existence.  

For negatives, add không before it: không có = “don’t have / there isn’t.”  
For yes–no questions, put có … không? around the main verb, or just raise your tone with có.  

Memory tip: think of có as the opposite of không có — “have” vs. “not have / no.”
